title: ADSC mission profile (Compliance Matrix Generator / Regulatory Precheck input)
description: Input document for the ADSC research-grade compliance precheck. This schema file is DOCUMENTATION: validation is implemented manually with the Python standard library in check_compliance.py (spec v4.2 R9 forbids pip dependencies such as jsonschema), so the two are dual-maintained - keep them in sync by hand. The precheck output is not legal advice. Unknown or missing fields are never treated as passing (unknown is safer than a false PASS).

mission_name.description: Human-readable profile name; appears in the findings and matrix.

mission_type.description: research_simulation = paper/software study only (no launch, no operations); flight = an actual mission. Most binding launch/licensing rules apply only to flight.

research_only.description: True if the profile describes research outputs only (no operational products). Mirrors the WP5 campaign metadata column research_only.

launching_state.description: Launching State in the Outer Space Treaty / Liability Convention sense, or a 'none_*'/'unspecified_*' marker for research profiles.

operating_state.description: State whose authorization/continuing supervision (OST Art. VI) would cover operations, or a research marker.

registry_state.description: State of registry (Registration Convention) for the servicer, or a research marker.

target_owner_state.description: State owning / exercising jurisdiction and control over the target object (OST Art. VIII). Class-level markers only for research profiles - never a specific catalog ID (spec D11/R13).

target_registered_state.description: State of registry of the target object, or a class-level research marker.

target_owner_consent.description: Affirmative consent of the target's owner/registry state for active interference. For research profiles this is a declared SCENARIO ASSUMPTION (spec D9), never a legal fact - the precheck records it as such. Absence of an affirmative true is treated as no consent (safe direction).

active_debris_removal.description: True if the mission concept performs active debris removal (attach/deorbit of a non-cooperative object).

proximity_operations.description: True if the concept includes rendezvous/proximity operations with another space object.

payload_has_transmitter.description: True if an RF transmitter is part of the modelled payload (triggers ITU/national frequency precheck).

payload_has_remote_sensing.description: True if an imaging/remote-sensing payload is modelled (triggers the NOAA placeholder precheck when applicable).

controlled_reentry.description: True if a controlled (targeted) reentry is part of the disposal concept. Mirrors WP5 column controlled_reentry_mode.

post_mission_disposal_lifetime_years.description: Design post-mission disposal lifetime in years for the LEO object(s). Checked against the IADC 25-year guideline and, for FCC-jurisdiction flight scenarios, the 5-year rule (FCC 22-74, 2022).

uses_live_tle.description: True if live ephemerides/TLEs are ingested anywhere. ADSC policy (spec D11/R13) requires false.

generates_target_specific_operations.description: True if target-specific operational planning products are generated. ADSC policy (spec D11/R13) requires false.

insurance_or_indemnification_evidence.description: Marker for third-party liability arrangements (e.g. third_party_liability_insurance, state_indemnification_agreement, self_insurance_documented) or a 'none_*' research marker.

export_control_review_status.description: Status of the export-control review (e.g. not_started, in_progress, reviewed_no_controlled_content, reviewed_with_authorization, not_required_confirmed_by_counsel). Anything other than a completed review yields a WARN.

evidence_documents.description: Tags of evidence documents the profile can point to. Rules list required tags in required_evidence; a required tag missing from this list yields WARN or BLOCK depending on rule severity.
